subject: Computer Networking
title: Network TAPs and Packet Brokers
description: A micro-lesson that makes Network TAPs and Packet Brokers usable.

hook.text: How do you monitor traffic on a wire without disrupting it? Install a TAP — a test access point.

buildup.text: A TAP copies every packet on a link and sends the copy to a monitoring tool. Original traffic flows untouched.

discovery.text: TAPs are passive. They add no latency, don't crash, and can't be detected by devices on the link.

twist.text: Packet brokers aggregate feeds from many TAPs, then filter, deduplicate, and distribute to monitoring tools.

climax.text: Security teams use TAPs for intrusion detection. Network teams use them to troubleshoot performance.

punchline.text: TAPs see every packet without touching traffic. Invisible insight.

quiz.question: Why are network TAPs preferred for monitoring?
options: They copy traffic passively without adding latency or risk
They compress traffic to save storage space
